title: Palette expansion — 4-channel → 8-channel + per-part overrides + derived bottom-shadow tints
intent: S101's BomberPalette is `{ head, torso, limbs, accent }` — 4 colour channels for 6 visible parts. That's enough to read 'this is a green bomber vs a red bomber' but loses detail: both arms share the same colour as both legs (single `limbs` channel), no way to distinguish hand from forearm, no separate helmet/visor channel, no dedicated emissive colour. This story expands the palette to 8 channels mapped 1:1 onto the post-decomposition mesh tree (GDP-2026-05-21-001), adds derived bottom-shadow tints for the contact-shadow-shader trick from visual-style.md §3, and lets recipes override individual channels (so unlocks / cosmetics can paint just the helmet without touching the rest).
rationale: Source: `docs/game-design/visual-style.md` §5 (project-wide colour language) + `docs/game-design/characters-and-visual.md` §4.2 (the 8-hue ring already used for bomber identity but flattened to 4 channels in S101). The S101 palette table is keyed by 8 named families (sky/ember/mint/plum/sand/jade/rose/slate) which is great for identity — but inside each family, the channel set is too coarse for visual variety. Expanding to 8 channels keeps the family identity (each family still has one canonical hue) while giving the mesh more places to differ. The bottom-shadow tint is the fake-contact-shadow trick that lets us drop the real shadow-map pass per visual-style.md §4.3.
acceptanceHints: Depends on GDP-2026-05-21-001 (the channels map to multi-mesh node names; merged-geometry palette is single-vertex-colour-per-part and can't carry 8 channels cleanly).
BomberPalette becomes 8 channels (was 4):
head            — head mesh primary colour (was `head`)
helmet          — secondary tint on top half of head; NEW. Set equal to `head` for hatless variants.
torso           — torso mesh primary colour (was `torso`)
torsoAccent     — secondary stripe / panel on torso; NEW. Set to torso × 0.7 for subtle variant, or to a distinct hue for chest-emblem effect.
upperLimb       — upperArm + upperLeg mesh colour (was half of `limbs`)
lowerLimb       — forearm + lowerLeg colour, slightly different from upperLimb for joint readability; was the other half of `limbs`
handFoot        — hand sphere + foot sphere/box colour, distinct from lowerLimb so the silhouette ends in a readable cap; NEW
accent          — emissive cyan or family-accent (was `accent`); used by accessories that emit (antenna tip, eye dot)

Each of the 8 channels has a DERIVED `bottomShadow` variant = channel × 0.6 (in linear RGB). The bottom 25% of each mesh's vertex colours sample the bottomShadow variant, the top 75% samples the channel. This is the fake-contact-shadow trick from visual-style.md §4.3 — costs zero perf, sells volume.

Update bomber-palette.ts: each of the 8 named families (sky/ember/mint/plum/sand/jade/rose/slate) gets 8 explicit channels. Family identity stays primary-hue-driven, but each family also picks deterministic shifts for the supporting channels (e.g. `mint` family: head = mint-primary, helmet = mint-darker, torso = mint-mid, torsoAccent = mint-cream, upperLimb = mint-mid, lowerLimb = mint-light, handFoot = mint-cream, accent = cyan-fixed).

Recipe palette override (the 'unlocks / cosmetics' lever): CharacterRecipe.palette is now `Partial<BomberPalette>` instead of an all-or-nothing replacement. A recipe with `palette: { helmet: '#ff00aa' }` keeps the family's other 7 channels intact but recolours the helmet. This unblocks the cosmetic-unlock plan in characters-and-visual.md §6.3 — unlocks paint single channels, not whole bombers.

Validator: each channel must parse as a 6-digit hex. The cross-channel contrast check (helmet vs head, accent vs background) lives in a separate optional QA helper, not in the validator — runtime accepts any palette, QA flags unreadable ones.

Bench UI: the palette dropdown stays (picks family). A new 'override' panel exposes 8 colour swatches; clicking a swatch opens a native `<input type="color">` so per-channel overrides are testable from the bench. 'Reset to family' button clears the override.

Generator integration: paintVertexColors() in bomber-mesh becomes paintPartVertexColors(part, channelColor, bottomShadowColor) where each box's vertex colours are split into bottom 25% (shadow) and top 75% (channel). For the box geometries used in v0, that's a per-vertex y-position test — trivial.

Unit tests: 8 named families have well-formed 8-channel palettes (all hex, all parseable). Bottom-shadow derivation is deterministic (channel × 0.6 in linear RGB; test the rounding). Per-channel recipe overrides apply cleanly without polluting the family.

Visual regression test: render 8 families × default-recipe in the bench → 8 screenshots, each distinct, all readable on the cool-dark-teal floor (visual-style.md §5.2 colour). This is a manual QA bar, not automated, documented in the bench README.
Performance: no draw-call change. The vertex-colour attribute is the same buffer shape, just populated with two colours instead of one per box. Build cost ~negligible.

Cosmetic-unlock pipeline (forward-looking, NOT in this story's scope but unblocked by it): future unlock systems write `recipe.palette.helmet = '#xxxx'` to a player's persistent profile. The unlock store schema is a separate proposal once persistent profile lands.

Out of scope (explicit): UV-mapped image textures, normal maps, roughness maps, environment maps. The palette + bottom-shadow is the full texturing strategy in this story; richer texturing (patterns, decals, panel seams) lands in GDP-2026-05-21-004.
